ddls:
CREATE TABLE "power_generation_technologies" (
  "technology_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the power generation technology */,
  "technology" TEXT /* Name of the power generation technology */,
  "technology_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the power generation technology */,
  "fuel_type" TEXT /* Type of fuel used by the power generation technology */,
  "cost_year" INTEGER /* Year for which the cost data is applicable */,
  "online_year" INTEGER /* Year when the technology went online */,
  "size_mw" INTEGER /* Size of the power plant in megawatts */,
  "leadtimes_years" INTEGER /* Lead time in years for constructing the power plant */,
  "base_overnight_costs" REAL /* Base cost for constructing the power plant overnight */,
  "project_contingency_factor" REAL /* Contingency factor for project cost overruns */,
  "technological_optimism_factor" REAL /* Optimism factor reflecting technological advancements */,
  "total_overnight_cost" REAL /* Total overnight cost considering all factors */,
  "variable_om" REAL /* Variable operation and maintenance cost per kilowatt */,
  "fixed_om" REAL /* Fixed operation and maintenance cost per kilowatt */,
  "heatrate_2008" INTEGER /* Heat rate in the year 2008 in British thermal units per kilowatt-hour */,
  "heatrate_nth_of_a_kind" INTEGER /* Heat rate for a nth-of-a-kind power plant in British thermal units per kilowatt-hour */,
  "efficiency_metrics" REAL /* Additional efficiency metrics, such as capacity factor or availability factor */,
  "construction_cost_breakdown" REAL /* Breakdown of construction costs, including labor, materials, and equipment costs */,
  "operation_and_maintenance_cost_breakdown" REAL /* Breakdown of operation and maintenance costs, including labor, materials, and equipment costs */,
  "emissions_data" REAL /* Emissions data, such as CO2 emissions per kilowatt-hour */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("technology_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user, such as administrator or analyst */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "scenario_plans" (
  "scenario_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the scenario plan */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user who created the scenario plan */,
  "scenario_name" TEXT /* Name of the scenario plan */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the scenario plan */,
  "variables" TEXT /* Variables used in the scenario plan, such as discount rates or inflation rates */,
  "assumptions" TEXT /* Assumptions made in the scenario plan */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the scenario plan was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the scenario plan was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scenario_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "technology_specifications" (
  "specification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the technical specification */,
  "technology_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the power generation technology */,
  "equipment" TEXT /* Equipment required for the technology */,
  "materials" TEXT /* Materials required for the technology */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the specification was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the specification was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("specification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "scenario_planning_variables" (
  "variable_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the scenario planning variable */,
  "scenario_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the scenario plan */,
  "variable_name" TEXT /* Name of the variable */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the variable */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the variable was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the variable was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("variable_id")
)
